channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Overpass Reflect on Covering Harry Styles and Their Evolving Sound
item.description: Overpass, the Birmingham-based indie band, have discussed how their cover of Harry Styles' 'American Girls' is influencing their approach to songwriting and shaping their new musical direction. During...

item.title: Cape Town Mayor Announces Mosque as New Buyer of Good Hope Centre After Church Bid Falls Through
item.description: The mayor of Cape Town, Geordin Hill-Lewis, disclosed during a live radio interview that the Good Hope Centre, an iconic property in the city, will not be purchased by the Nigerian church that initial...

item.title: sophie cunningham's sister lindsey: basketball career and family legacy
item.description: the article highlights the achievements of lindsey cunningham, sophie cunningham's older sister, who built a notable basketball career at missouri university before transitioning to coaching and medic...

item.title: Three suspects arrested in Negros Oriental marine biologist's murder case
item.description: Three suspects were arrested in connection with the slaying of a marine biologist in Negros Oriental, according to a report by ABS-CBN News. The incident, which occurred on July 16, 2026, has sparked ...

item.title: Samuel Egube’s contributions to Lagos governance and development celebrated by elite group
item.description: Samuel Egube, the deputy chief of staff to Lagos State Governor Babajide Sanwo-Olu, is set to be honoured by the Urhobo and Isoko Elites for his significant contributions to public service and governa...

item.title: Pakistan Joins China-Led AI Governance Body as Founding Member
item.description: Deputy Prime Minister Ishaq Dar is set to finalize Pakistan's membership in the World Artificial Intelligence Cooperation Organisation (WAICO), a China-backed initiative aimed at global AI governance....

item.title: Chadema Takes Strong Stance Against Defectors to Strengthen Party Unity
item.description: Chadema, Tanzania's main opposition party, has announced new measures to address internal defections to rival parties. The party aims to curb disloyalty by implementing a 'kitanzi' strategy, which inv...

item.title: Former Essendon Player Supports Hird's Coaching Bid Amid Team Challenges
item.description: Jake Melksham, a former Essendon player and current Melbourne Demon, has publicly endorsed James Hird's bid to return as coach of the Bombers. Melksham, who played alongside Hird during his time at Es...

item.title: Tanzania's Women Face Rising Online Harassment, Calls for Safer Digital Spaces
item.description: A recent study reveals that nearly six out of ten young women in Tanzania have experienced online harassment, driving many to withdraw from social media. Conducted by WiLDAF, UNFPA, and Tanzania Commu...

item.title: US Imposes 25% Tariff on Brazil Imports Amid Trade Disputes
item.description: The United States has announced a new 25% tariff on specific imports from Brazil, effective July 22, following an investigation into trade practices. A senior US official confirmed the move, stating t...

item.title: Boda Boda Rider's Body Found in River After 3 Weeks Missing
item.description: James Gichohi Wanjau, a boda boda rider from Githumu Village in Murang'a County, was reported missing on 28 June 2026. His mutilated body was allegedly recovered from a river, with his head found sepa...

item.title: DOJ assures NBI's professional investigation into SEA Games infrastructure
item.description: The Department of Justice (DOJ) has assured the National Bureau of Investigation (NBI) will conduct a thorough and impartial probe into allegations of infrastructural irregularities during the 2019 So...

item.title: Global CKD Diagnosis Crisis: Over 400 Million Cases Remain Undiagnosed, Warns Lancet Study
item.description: A recent study published in The Lancet reveals that nearly half of the estimated 844 million chronic kidney disease (CKD) cases worldwide remain undiagnosed, with higher rates in low- and middle-incom...

item.title: Jozi FM Governance Dispute Escalates as Legal Battle Over Board Control Intensifies
item.description: A legal battle over the governance of Jozi FM has reignited in the Gauteng High Court, with director Rapitse Peter Montsho challenging the authority of a rival board to manage the community radio stat...

item.title: Pakistan Urges US and Iran to Resume Talks to Ease Regional Tensions
item.description: Pakistan's Foreign Office spokesperson Tahir Hussain Andrabi has emphasized that the country will continue to encourage the United States and Iran to stop violence and resume technical-level talks und...

item.title: Alberta Court Extends Asset Freeze on Separatist Lawyer in Legal Dispute with First Nation
item.description: A Alberta judge has extended and expanded a temporary order freezing the assets of Jeff Rath, a leader of the province’s separatist movement, in a legal dispute with the Tallcree First Nation. The ord...

item.title: Nasir El-Rufai's 150-day Detention Sparks Concern Over Lawfare in Nigeria
item.description: The article discusses the prolonged detention of Nasir El-Rufai, a prominent Nigerian politician and critic of President Tinubu, who has been held for 150 days. His wife, Ahmad El-Rufai, highlights th...

item.title: Ontario adopts unified regulatory framework for public companies
item.description: Ontario has finalized its agreement to join Canada's 'passport' system for publicly traded companies, marking the end of its long-standing push for a national securities regulator. This decision resol...

item.title: Tuchel Refuses to Blame Curse After England's Semi-Final Loss to Argentina
item.description: England's football team faced another setback in their World Cup campaign as they lost to Argentina in the semi-finals, extending their wait for a major trophy. Manager Thomas Tuchel addressed the def...

item.title: Sudbury Launches Youth Mental Health Program This Fall
item.description: A new youth mental health initiative is set to launch in Sudbury, Ontario, this fall, aiming to address rising concerns about adolescent well-being in the region. The program, spearheaded by local hea...
